cards.headline: Iran tensions shake oil prices and global stocks
cards.summary: Crude oil prices jumped and stock prices fell after President Trump declared an end to the ceasefire with Iran, adding fresh uncertainty to an already shaky global economy. Markets partially recovered as investors assessed prospects for peace, with the S&P 500, Dow, and Nasdaq later rising as focus shifted to AI developments. The episode highlights how quickly geopolitical flashpoints can rattle financial markets.

quiz.prompt: What triggered the initial jump in crude oil prices described in these reports?
options: Trump declaring an end to the ceasefire with Iran
A major refinery outage in the Gulf
quiz.explanation: NPR reported that crude oil prices jumped after President Trump declared an end to the ceasefire with Iran, adding uncertainty to the global economy.

cards.headline: Stock market minted nearly 1 million new millionaires in 2025
cards.summary: Global personal wealth surged by 10.8% in 2025, the biggest jump since 2017, according to a new UBS report. Stock market gains were the primary driver, minting nearly one million new millionaires over the period. The figures underscore a widening gap in financial outcomes, as a separate poll found roughly half of Americans struggle to afford groceries and gas.

quiz.prompt: According to UBS, how much did global personal wealth grow in 2025?
quiz.explanation: The UBS report cited by CNBC found global personal wealth surged by 10.8% in 2025, the biggest jump since 2017.

cards.headline: Half of Americans struggle to afford groceries and gas
cards.summary: About half of Americans report struggling to afford groceries and gas, according to an exclusive Harris poll for the Guardian. Approximately 57% of those surveyed also believe the economy is worsening, painting a grim picture of the cost of living crisis. The findings come even as stock market wealth has hit record highs, highlighting a deeply uneven economic landscape.

quiz.prompt: What share of Americans polled by Harris for the Guardian said they believe the economy is worsening?
quiz.explanation: The Guardian's exclusive Harris poll found about 57% of Americans believe the economy is worsening.

cards.headline: Rivian shares drop 18% after major stock offering
cards.summary: Rivian's stock fell 18% after the company sold 75 million shares to raise capital, following a strong run that included gains of 19.2% the prior week and 8.1% on Monday. The EV maker simultaneously raised its 2026 delivery outlook to between 65,000 and 70,000 vehicles, up from a prior range of 62,000 to 67,000. The news came as Ford reported its own EV sales fell 40.7% year-over-year in Q2, adding to a mixed picture for the electric vehicle sector.

quiz.prompt: By how much did Rivian raise its 2026 EV delivery outlook?
quiz.explanation: CNBC reported Rivian raised its delivery outlook to between 65,000 and 70,000 EVs, up from its prior range of 62,000 to 67,000.

cards.headline: Toyota invests $3.6bn to shift Tacoma production to Texas
cards.summary: Toyota announced a $3.6 billion investment to move production of its Tacoma midsize pickup truck from Mexico to its San Antonio, Texas manufacturing campus. The move comes as USMCA trade talks reopen, with Ford's CEO also calling for a level playing field with Toyota and GM imports. Ford separately reported assembling over 2 million vehicles in the U.S. last year, more than any other automaker.

quiz.prompt: Where is Toyota moving Tacoma production from Mexico to?
quiz.explanation: Toyota announced it is investing $3.6 billion to move Tacoma production to its San Antonio, Texas manufacturing campus.

cards.headline: EasyJet agrees in principle to £5.5bn US takeover
cards.summary: EasyJet has said it intends to accept a £5.5 billion takeover bid from US investment firm Castlelake, following several previously rejected offers. The deal would take the UK's biggest low-cost carrier private. The agreement in principle marks a significant shift for one of Europe's most recognisable budget airlines.

quiz.prompt: Which US firm has EasyJet agreed in principle to be taken over by?
quiz.explanation: The Guardian reported EasyJet intends to accept a £5.5bn takeover offer from US investment firm Castlelake.

cards.headline: Volkswagen faces protests over 100,000 job cut plan
cards.summary: Volkswagen's proposal to slash up to 100,000 jobs and close factories faced a major test as demonstrations broke out at 18 sites across Germany. The protests came as a radical transformation plan was put to the company's board. VW is Europe's biggest carmaker, and the scale of the proposed cuts has alarmed workers and unions.

quiz.prompt: How many jobs does Volkswagen's proposed transformation plan aim to cut?
quiz.explanation: The Guardian reported Volkswagen's proposal involves slashing up to 100,000 jobs and closing factories, prompting protests at 18 sites.

cards.headline: Major drugmaker loses $27bn after heart drug trial fails
cards.summary: A major drugmaker lost $27 billion in market value after a heart-disease drug it had been co-developing failed its Phase 3 clinical trial. Two companies jointly announced the setback. The collapse in market value illustrates the high financial stakes of late-stage drug development.

quiz.prompt: At what stage did the heart-disease drug fail its clinical trial?
quiz.explanation: MarketWatch reported the heart-disease drug failed its Phase 3 trial, wiping $27 billion from the drugmaker's market value.

cards.headline: Manhattan office leasing hits strongest gains in 20 years
cards.summary: Office leasing volumes in Manhattan during the second quarter were 31.3% above the 10-year average, according to a new report from Colliers, marking the strongest gains in 20 years. Separately, Manhattan luxury real estate sales held firm despite fears of a so-called 'Mamdani effect' following a new tax on second homes in New York City. Both data points suggest commercial and high-end residential demand in Manhattan remains resilient.

quiz.prompt: How far above the 10-year average were Manhattan office leasing volumes in Q2, per Colliers?
quiz.explanation: CNBC reported that Manhattan office leasing volumes in Q2 were 31.3% above the 10-year average, the strongest gain in 20 years.

cards.headline: Dollar's historic bond-market buffer is fading, analysts warn
cards.summary: A historic bond-market buffer that has long protected the US dollar is fading, according to MarketWatch, as major dislocations in American foreign policy accelerate a dedollarization trend among central banks. Separately, Charles Schwab strategists warned that the era of easy index-fund gains is over, citing more frequent geopolitical instability, supply shocks, and macro volatility. Together the signals point to a more challenging environment for US investors accustomed to steady market tailwinds.

quiz.prompt: What trend among central banks is the fading bond-market buffer helping to accelerate, per MarketWatch?
options: A move to digital central bank currencies
quiz.explanation: MarketWatch reported that dislocations in US foreign policy have given stimulus to a dedollarization trend among central banks as the dollar's bond-market buffer fades.

cards.headline: Parents warned as AI turns kids' photos into abuse material
cards.summary: The UK's National Crime Agency and safety watchdogs are urging parents to stop publicly sharing children's images amid a growing threat of nudification apps being used to create child sexual abuse material. Meta's move to allow AI-generated images from public Instagram profiles has amplified concerns, with privacy campaigners calling it a 'recipe for disaster.' Authorities say the scale and sophistication of such tools is increasing rapidly.

quiz.prompt: Why are authorities urging parents not to publicly share children's images?
options: AI tools can use those images to create sexual abuse material
Social media platforms sell the data to advertisers
School records can be accessed by hackers
quiz.explanation: The NCA and safety watchdogs warn that AI nudification apps are increasingly being used to turn publicly shared children's images into explicit abuse material.

cards.headline: Microsoft cuts 4,800 jobs, shrinks Xbox significantly
cards.summary: Microsoft is conducting a major restructuring, cutting 4,800 jobs — about 2.1% of its total workforce — with 1,600 of those losses hitting Xbox immediately. Bethesda and id Software are also reported to be hard-hit, with some teams losing as much as 50% of their staff. The company has described the move as a 'significant restructure.'

quiz.prompt: What percentage of Microsoft's workforce does the 4,800-job cut represent?
quiz.explanation: According to reports, the 4,800 layoffs equate to approximately 2.1% of Microsoft's total workforce.

cards.headline: AI tools quietly altering users' messages on sensitive topics
cards.summary: A new study has found that AI drafting tools are subtly changing the meaning of users' messages on sensitive political subjects such as abortion and climate change. Researchers warn that even small changes in phrasing could spread rapidly at scale and create long-term shifts in public opinion. The findings raise fresh questions about AI's role in shaping political discourse.

quiz.prompt: What concern did researchers raise about AI drafting tools?
options: They are too slow for practical use
They alter the meaning of messages on sensitive political topics
They expose private user data to third parties
They generate misinformation about scientific facts only
quiz.explanation: Researchers found that AI tools make small but meaningful changes to drafts on sensitive topics like abortion and climate, which could shift public opinion at scale.

cards.headline: US partially lifts export ban on Anthropic's top AI model
cards.summary: The Trump administration has partially lifted an export ban on Anthropic's most advanced AI model, asserting a new level of government influence over which companies can access cutting-edge AI. OpenAI has also agreed to let the administration screen users of its latest model. The moves signal a significant expansion of federal oversight over frontier AI systems.

quiz.prompt: What did OpenAI agree to allow under the new US AI oversight approach?
options: The administration to own a stake in the company
Government to screen users of its new model
A full ban on international sales
Public access to its model weights
quiz.explanation: According to reports, OpenAI agreed to let the Trump administration screen users of its new model as part of expanded federal oversight of frontier AI.

cards.headline: Google ordered to pay €4.1bn Android antitrust fine
cards.summary: A court has ruled that Google must pay a €4.1 billion fine for using its Android operating system to block rivals. A Google spokesperson said the judgment 'fails to recognise' the firm's 'significant investment to ensure Android remains open.' The ruling is one of the largest antitrust penalties ever levied against a tech company.

quiz.prompt: Why was Google fined €4.1 billion?
options: For data privacy violations involving European users
For using Android to block rivals
For anti-competitive pricing on the Play Store
For failing to pay EU digital services taxes
quiz.explanation: The court found that Google used its Android platform to block rival companies, resulting in the €4.1 billion fine.

cards.headline: Australia's child social media ban barely affecting access
cards.summary: A new study finds that six months after Australia introduced a world-first social media ban for children, youth access to these platforms has barely changed. Australia is now planning to double potential fines for platforms like Facebook and Instagram that fail to prevent children from holding accounts. The findings raise doubts about whether legislative bans alone can effectively curb children's social media use.

quiz.prompt: What does the study about Australia's child social media ban conclude?
options: Children's access has been fully eliminated
The ban has barely affected youth access to social media
Fines have deterred all major platforms
Children have shifted entirely to gaming platforms
quiz.explanation: Despite Australia's world-first ban and promises of tougher penalties, a new study found that six months in, children's access to social media has barely changed.

cards.headline: Supreme Court lets Texas app store age law stand
cards.summary: The US Supreme Court has allowed Texas's App Store Accountability Act to take effect while legal challenges continue in lower courts. The law requires minors to obtain parental permission before downloading most apps. Major tech companies have called the measure a 'censorship regime,' but the court declined to block it for now.

quiz.prompt: What does Texas's App Store Accountability Act require?
options: App stores to remove all adult content
Minors to have parental permission to download most apps
Tech companies to pay a licensing fee to operate in Texas
All apps to display age ratings prominently
quiz.explanation: The Texas law requires minors to get parental permission before downloading most apps, and the Supreme Court has let it take effect while lawsuits proceed.

cards.headline: Meta uses public Instagram pics to generate AI images
cards.summary: Meta has announced it can use images from public Instagram profiles to generate AI-created content, with an opt-out option for users who do not want their photos used this way. Privacy campaigners have called the policy a 'recipe for disaster,' warning it creates new risks for people who may not be aware their images are being used. The move comes as Meta also faces scrutiny over its AI glasses data collection practices.

quiz.prompt: How can Instagram users avoid having their public photos used for Meta's AI image generation?
options: By making their account private or opting out
There is no way to avoid it
quiz.explanation: Meta said users can opt out of having their public Instagram images used for AI generation, though privacy advocates have criticised this approach.

cards.headline: Trump program fast-tracks new small nuclear reactor designs
cards.summary: A program initiated by the Trump administration has allowed small companies to accelerate testing of several new nuclear reactor designs. Some experts worry that safety standards are being compromised in the rush to bring new reactor concepts to market. Separately, Miami-based City Labs achieved a first for commercial nuclear power deployed in space.

quiz.prompt: What concern have some experts raised about the Trump administration's nuclear reactor program?
options: Reactors are being built too far from population centres
Safety may be compromised by the accelerated pace
Costs are far higher than conventional plants
quiz.explanation: Some experts worry that the Trump administration's push to fast-track new nuclear reactor designs is compromising safety standards.

cards.headline: Ivy League exam scores drop 50% when AI cheating blocked
cards.summary: A professor at an Ivy League institution suspected widespread AI cheating and ordered students to take an in-person final exam instead; scores fell by around 50% compared to prior assessments. The professor warned that AI cheating leads to 'a failed society.' The incident has reignited debate about the integrity of remote and AI-assisted academic work.

quiz.prompt: What happened to exam scores when an Ivy League professor required an in-person final to prevent AI cheating?
options: The exam was cancelled due to low turnout
quiz.explanation: When the professor switched to an in-person exam to block AI use, student scores dropped by around 50%, suggesting widespread prior reliance on AI tools.

cards.headline: US strikes Iran; Tehran fires back at Gulf states
cards.summary: The US military struck 90 Iranian targets in the latest round of airstrikes, with Iran's health ministry reporting 14 people killed since Tuesday. Iran responded by targeting Bahrain, Kuwait, and Qatar, triggering air raid sirens across Gulf states. Neither side has officially called off an interim peace agreement, but President Trump said he believes the ceasefire is dead.

quiz.prompt: How many Iranian targets did the US military say it struck in the latest round of airstrikes?
quiz.explanation: Centcom stated it hit 90 Iranian targets in the latest round of strikes.

cards.headline: NATO summit ends with Ukraine Patriot deal, $49bn missile project
cards.summary: President Trump capped the NATO summit in Turkey by meeting Ukrainian President Zelenskyy and announcing that the US will give Ukraine a licence to produce Patriot air defence systems. NATO allies also announced £37 billion for a new missile project, with Prime Minister Starmer convening around a dozen leaders to discuss the programme in Ankara. Trump's attacks on Spain and Greenland raised questions about alliance unity even as members agreed to boost defence spending.

quiz.prompt: What did Trump announce the US would give Ukraine at the NATO summit?
options: A shipment of ready-made Patriot missiles
A licence to produce Patriot air defence systems
quiz.explanation: Trump said the US would give Ukraine a licence to produce Patriot air defence missiles, though they have lengthy production times.

cards.headline: Platner drops Senate bid after assault allegation
cards.summary: Democrat Graham Platner suspended his campaign for the Maine US Senate seat after a woman accused him of sexual assault, an allegation he called "categorically false." Top Democrats, including Bernie Sanders, withdrew support, and the party now has until July 27 to replace him in the key battleground race against Republican Senator Susan Collins. Several potential successors have already signalled interest, including Nirav Shah, a former Democratic gubernatorial candidate.

quiz.prompt: Who is the Republican incumbent that Maine Democrats were hoping to unseat in the Senate race?
quiz.explanation: Graham Platner was running to unseat GOP Senator Susan Collins in Maine.

cards.headline: UN probe finds RSF committed genocide in Darfur
cards.summary: A UN Fact-Finding Mission found that Sudan's Rapid Support Forces carried out mass killings and gang rapes amounting to genocide in Darfur. The ICC separately told the BBC of a breakthrough in its Sudan war crimes probe, which has been ongoing for three years. The findings significantly raise the international legal stakes around the conflict.

quiz.prompt: Which body found that Sudan's RSF committed genocide in Darfur?
quiz.explanation: A UN Fact-Finding Mission determined that the RSF's systematic campaign of violence in Darfur amounted to genocide.

cards.headline: Khamenei burial proceeds as Iran strikes Gulf neighbours
cards.summary: Funeral ceremonies for Iran's late Supreme Leader Khamenei continued for a fifth day, with his remains transported to the Shia shrines of Najaf and Karbala in Iraq. The burial is set to take place in his hometown, the eastern holy city of Mashhad. The ceremonies are unfolding simultaneously with Iran's military escalation against the US and Gulf states.

quiz.prompt: Where is Iran's late Supreme Leader Khamenei set to be buried?
quiz.explanation: According to the snippets, Khamenei will be buried in his hometown, the eastern holy city of Mashhad.

cards.headline: Andy Burnham frontrunner to become UK's next PM
cards.summary: UK Labour leadership nominations have begun, with former Greater Manchester Mayor Andy Burnham emerging as the clear frontrunner. If no rival candidate emerges, Burnham could become Prime Minister this month. NPR profiled how Burnham's background and his tenure growing Manchester have shaped his political identity ahead of a potential national role.

quiz.prompt: What role did Andy Burnham hold before becoming a frontrunner for UK Labour leader?
quiz.explanation: Andy Burnham served as Mayor of Manchester before emerging as the frontrunner in the UK Labour leadership race.

cards.headline: IOC clears Russian athletes to return to Olympics
cards.summary: The International Olympic Committee has cleared the way for Russian athletes to return to Olympic competition, effectively overturning a decade-long ban imposed following a state-sponsored doping scandal and Russia's full-scale invasion of Ukraine. The decision is set to prove deeply controversial among athletes and governments who backed the ban. No conditions detailed in the snippets were attached to the reinstatement.

quiz.prompt: Why had Russian athletes faced a decade-long ban from Olympic competition?
options: A state-sponsored doping scandal and Russia's invasion of Ukraine
quiz.explanation: The ban was imposed due to a state-sponsored doping scandal and Russia's full-scale invasion of Ukraine, according to the snippets.

cards.headline: Major Telstra outage hits trains, emergency calls in Australia
cards.summary: A major outage at Australia's largest telecoms company, Telstra, disrupted train services and emergency triple-zero calls, with servers at data centres in Sydney and Melbourne identified as the cause. South Australian police opened an investigation into the death of an elderly person that a senator claimed was linked to the outage. The exact cause of the server failure remains unknown.

quiz.prompt: Which Australian telecoms company suffered the major outage affecting emergency calls and trains?
quiz.explanation: The outage occurred at Telstra, Australia's largest telecoms company, disrupting trains and triple-zero emergency calls.

cards.headline: Canada picks Germany to build 12 cutting-edge submarines
cards.summary: Canada has selected a German consortium to build a dozen cutting-edge submarines in what is described as one of the country's largest defence contracts. The deal deepens Canada's NATO ties and comes after a bidding war that saw a South Korean rival defeated. The contract is worth multiple billions of dollars.

quiz.prompt: How many submarines is Canada purchasing from the German consortium?
quiz.explanation: Canada selected a German consortium to build a dozen — 12 — cutting-edge submarines.

cards.headline: Australia to sell uranium to India for peaceful use
cards.summary: Australia and India have struck a deal for Australia to begin selling uranium to India for peaceful purposes, announced during Indian Prime Minister Modi's visit. India has long sought Australian uranium to help meet a target of 100 gigawatts of nuclear energy capacity by 2047. Modi also addressed a crowd of 25,000 Indian diaspora members at Marvel Stadium in Melbourne.

quiz.prompt: What nuclear energy capacity target is India aiming to reach by 2047?
quiz.explanation: India has a target of 100 gigawatts of nuclear energy capacity by 2047, which is why it has long sought Australian uranium.

cards.headline: Fery becomes first British wildcard in Wimbledon semis
cards.summary: Arthur Fery defeated Italy's Flavio Cobolli in straight sets to become the first British wildcard and only the second player in Wimbledon history to reach the semi-finals. The British wildcard will face Germany's Alexander Zverev in his semi-final on Friday. Fery credited his self-belief and plans to use the home crowd to his advantage against Zverev.

quiz.prompt: Who will Arthur Fery face in his Wimbledon semi-final?
quiz.explanation: According to the reports, Fery will face Germany's Alexander Zverev in his Wimbledon semi-final on Friday.

cards.headline: France face Morocco in World Cup quarter-final today
cards.summary: France and Morocco meet in a 2026 World Cup quarter-final on Thursday, with France the overwhelming favourites to win the tournament. France failed in their bid to have Michael Olise's yellow card overturned, while Morocco are without Ismael Saibari for the match. France coach Didier Deschamps has played down the appointment of Argentinian officials for the game.

quiz.prompt: Which France player's yellow card appeal was rejected ahead of the Morocco quarter-final?
quiz.explanation: France failed in their bid to have forward Michael Olise's yellow card overturned ahead of the quarter-final against Morocco.

cards.headline: Bruno Guimarães tells Newcastle he wants Arsenal move
cards.summary: Newcastle captain Bruno Guimarães has told the club he wants to join Arsenal, with the champions believed to be preparing a £60m offer. The development comes as a fresh blow to Newcastle, who have also lost Gordon and Tonali. Newcastle have insisted Guimarães is not for sale amid the speculation.

quiz.prompt: How much are Arsenal reportedly preparing to offer for Bruno Guimarães?
quiz.explanation: Reports indicate Arsenal are preparing a £60m offer for the Newcastle captain Bruno Guimarães.

cards.headline: Man Utd reveal 100,000-seat stadium location
cards.summary: Manchester United have revealed the location for their proposed new 100,000-seater stadium, situated approximately 350 metres north west of their existing Old Trafford ground. The club also agreed a £50m deal with Chelsea for Brazilian midfielder Andrey Santos. The twin announcements mark a significant week of activity at the club.

quiz.prompt: Where is Manchester United's proposed new stadium located relative to Old Trafford?
quiz.explanation: Manchester United revealed the proposed new 100,000-seater stadium will be located approximately 350 metres north west of the existing Old Trafford ground.

cards.headline: Republican lawmakers pressure WNBA over Clark fouls
cards.summary: Eleven Republican lawmakers sent a letter to WNBA commissioner Cathy Engelbert demanding the league take accountability for what they described as possible racially motivated attacks on Indiana Fever star Caitlin Clark. Clark returned to action but was limited to 16 minutes under a minutes cap, finishing with nine points in a 106-92 loss to the Sparks. The letter specifically cited concerns about hard fouls directed at Clark during her career.

quiz.prompt: How many Republican lawmakers signed the letter to the WNBA regarding Caitlin Clark?
quiz.explanation: Eleven Republican lawmakers sent a letter to WNBA commissioner Cathy Engelbert demanding accountability over treatment of Caitlin Clark.

cards.headline: ICC contacts ECB over Stokes retirement rule breach
cards.summary: The ICC contacted the England and Wales Cricket Board over a potential rule breach linked to Ben Stokes's retirement announcement. Stokes reacted to the news by saying 'sack him,' appearing to make light of the situation. The ICC's concerns relate to the manner in which the retirement was announced.

quiz.prompt: Which body contacted the ECB over a potential rule breach linked to Ben Stokes's retirement?
quiz.explanation: The ICC contacted the ECB over a potential rule breach connected to Ben Stokes's retirement announcement.

cards.headline: Kostyuk stuns defending champion Paolini at Wimbledon
cards.summary: Marta Kostyuk beat Jasmine Paolini 6-3, 6-2 to reach her first Wimbledon singles semi-final. Kostyuk will face Linda Noskova in the semi-finals, while Coco Gauff takes on Karolina Muchova in the other women's semi-final. The result was a significant upset against the defending champion Paolini.

quiz.prompt: What was the scoreline as Kostyuk defeated Paolini at Wimbledon?
quiz.explanation: Marta Kostyuk beat Jasmine Paolini 6-3, 6-2 to reach her first Wimbledon singles semi-final.

cards.headline: Sprint sensation Gout Gout out of World U20 Championships
cards.summary: Australian sprint sensation Gout Gout has withdrawn from the 2026 World Athletics Under-20 Championships after suffering a hamstring tear during training in Brisbane. The 18-year-old vowed to return in 2027 'better and faster and stronger.' The injury is a major blow ahead of what had been a highly anticipated appearance.

quiz.prompt: Why did Gout Gout withdraw from the World U20 Championships?
quiz.explanation: Gout Gout pulled out after suffering a hamstring tear during training in Brisbane.

cards.headline: ICC flags 'significant' corruption concerns in franchise cricket
cards.summary: The ICC is formulating plans to tackle what it has called significant corruption concerns in franchise cricket tournaments around the globe. The governing body described the issue as a growing and serious threat to the integrity of the sport. No specific tournaments were named in the reports.

quiz.prompt: What type of cricket tournaments has the ICC flagged for significant corruption concerns?
quiz.explanation: The ICC described significant corruption concerns specifically in franchise tournaments around the globe.

cards.headline: McGregor returns to face Holloway at UFC 329
cards.summary: Conor McGregor is set to headline UFC 329 against Max Holloway as part of International Fight Week in Las Vegas. The fight marks McGregor's return to the octagon and is one of the most anticipated bouts of the year. CBS Sports listed the McGregor-Holloway countdown as one of the major sports storylines of the day.

quiz.prompt: Who is Conor McGregor fighting at UFC 329?
quiz.explanation: Conor McGregor is set to headline UFC 329 against Max Holloway during International Fight Week in Las Vegas.

cards.headline: US strikes Iran second night as ceasefire collapses
cards.summary: US military forces carried out strikes on 90 targets in Iran, with a second night of attacks following a brief interim ceasefire. Iran retaliated with drone strikes on US-linked sites in Gulf states, triggering air raid sirens in Bahrain and missile interceptions over Jordan. Pakistan, acting as mediator, warned the renewed conflict is 'in no one's interest' as fears of wider regional escalation grow.

quiz.prompt: How many targets did the US military say it struck in Iran?
quiz.explanation: According to the snippets, the US military said it struck 90 targets in Iran during the renewed campaign.

cards.headline: Gulf states on alert as Iran-US strikes intensify
cards.summary: Air raid sirens sounded across Gulf states and videos showed Iranian missiles being intercepted over Jordan as the US-Iran conflict escalated. Iran's late supreme leader Khamenei was being prepared for burial as the country simultaneously launched retaliatory drone strikes. Neither side has officially called off their interim peace agreement despite the ongoing escalation.

quiz.prompt: Where were Iranian missiles filmed being intercepted during the latest escalation?
quiz.explanation: Videos showed the interception of Iranian missiles in the skies over Jordan, with sirens also blaring in several Jordanian cities.

cards.headline: UN finds Sudan's RSF committed genocide in Darfur
cards.summary: A UN Fact-Finding Mission has concluded that Sudan's Rapid Support Forces carried out a systematic campaign of mass killings and gang rapes in Darfur amounting to genocide. The findings mark a significant international legal and diplomatic escalation in the Sudan conflict. The paramilitary's conduct was described as systematic rather than isolated.

quiz.prompt: Which body concluded that Sudan's RSF committed genocide?
quiz.explanation: The UN Fact-Finding Mission found that the RSF's systematic campaign of violence in Darfur amounted to genocide.

cards.headline: Iran war accelerating global shift to clean energy
cards.summary: The renewed round of bombing in the Middle East has highlighted the risks of relying on fossil fuels, with experts saying the conflict is speeding up the global transition to electric vehicles, solar power, and batteries. Countries exposed to oil supply disruptions are increasingly looking to accelerate clean energy adoption. The crisis is being seen as a turning point for energy security policy worldwide.

quiz.prompt: According to experts, what is the Iran conflict speeding up globally?
options: The transition to EVs, solar, and batteries
quiz.explanation: Experts say the new round of bombing in the Middle East is speeding up the global transition to EVs, solar, and batteries by underscoring the risks of fossil fuel reliance.

cards.headline: Ten people fatally shot by immigration officials under Trump
cards.summary: At least 10 people have been fatally shot by immigration enforcement officials during Trump's second term, with the death of 52-year-old Lorenzo Salgado Araujo described as the latest high-profile killing by ICE officers. The cases have drawn significant scrutiny as Trump's immigration crackdown continues. Civil rights attorney Ben Crump has also been enlisted to help investigate the July Fourth weekend death of Nolan Wells.

quiz.prompt: How many people have been fatally shot by immigration officials during Trump's second term, according to reports?
quiz.explanation: The Guardian reported that 10 people have been fatally shot by immigration enforcement officials during Trump's second term.

cards.headline: Democrats scramble after Maine Senate candidate quits
cards.summary: Graham Platner ended his Democratic Senate campaign in Maine following a sexual assault allegation, announcing his withdrawal in a video accusing the party and media of acting as 'judge, jury and executioner.' Democrats are now scrambling to find a replacement candidate, with Maine Democrats planning a convention to select a new nominee. The meltdown is seen as putting the party in danger ahead of the midterms.

quiz.prompt: Why did Graham Platner end his Maine Senate campaign?
options: He faced a sexual assault allegation
He ran out of campaign funds
quiz.explanation: Platner ended his campaign after a sexual assault allegation, announcing his withdrawal in an angry video.

cards.headline: Andy Burnham on course to become UK prime minister
cards.summary: Former Greater Manchester Mayor Andy Burnham is the frontrunner to replace Sir Keir Starmer as Labour leader, with no rival candidate having emerged as nominations opened. If unchallenged, Burnham could become prime minister this month. His tenure as mayor, during which he brought growth to post-industrial Manchester, is being cited as a model for what he might do nationally.

quiz.prompt: Who is Andy Burnham set to replace as UK Labour leader?
quiz.explanation: Andy Burnham is the frontrunner to replace Sir Keir Starmer as Labour leader and, by extension, as prime minister.

cards.headline: Singer Bonnie Tyler dies unexpectedly aged 75
cards.summary: Welsh singer Bonnie Tyler, known for the iconic ballad 'Total Eclipse of the Heart,' has died aged 75. Her family said she died unexpectedly in a hospital in Portugal where she was being treated for an illness, issuing a statement on her website. Tyler was celebrated for her distinctive gravel-voiced style and her impact on music history.

quiz.prompt: Where did Bonnie Tyler die, according to her family's statement?
quiz.explanation: Tyler's family said she died unexpectedly in a hospital in Portugal where she was being treated for an illness.

cards.headline: Bangladesh measles outbreak kills hundreds of children
cards.summary: Bangladesh is reporting 120,000 measles cases, with hundreds of children dying and hospitals overwhelmed. The outbreak represents a dramatic reversal for a country that had previously made significant progress in combating the disease. The scale of the crisis has alarmed global health observers.

quiz.prompt: How many measles cases has Bangladesh reported in its current outbreak?
quiz.explanation: According to the BBC, Bangladesh is reporting 120,000 measles cases, with hospitals overwhelmed and hundreds of children dying.

cards.headline: US chemical accidents rising as Trump cuts protections
cards.summary: The number of chemical accidents in the United States is rising at the same time as the Trump administration is rolling back federal safety protections and gutting disaster management systems. Deaths and injuries from chemical incidents are up, according to a new report, prompting criticism of the administration's approach. Critics have described the plan to reduce federal oversight as 'appalling.'

quiz.prompt: What trend in chemical accidents is occurring alongside the Trump administration's rollback of protections?
quiz.explanation: The Guardian reported that deaths and injuries from US chemical accidents are up as the Trump administration rolls back federal safety protections.
